methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pasqal is a pre-IPO quantum computing company that has raised €340M+ to date. The broader quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and 8 private peers (including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion).

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing peer set, while containing 7 same-sector companies, lacks sufficient depth at Pasqal's specific stage and valuation tier to support reliable triangulation. No grouping of three or more comparables meeting narrow stage-alignment or similar-tier criteria could be established. A broad sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of business models, technology approaches, and maturity levels across quantum platforms.

This analysis cannot produce a point estimate or bounded range without either (1) expanding the dataset to include earlier or later-stage quantum peers with disclosed valuations, or (2) employing alternative methods such as DCF or venture capital method inputs. The €340M+ capital raised establishes a floor for stakeholder expectations but does not directly imply current valuation. Key uncertainties include quantum hardware/software differentiation, customer concentration, and the timing and structure of any public exit. Further diligence on comparable transaction multiples and revenue trajectories would be required to support a defensible range.
